issues: In four separate locations (vital/lib/reschedule.ts, wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts, bookings.tsx:553, handleCancelBooking.ts:458), async callbacks are used inside .forEach(), which does not await async callbacks. This means promises are fire-and-forget: surrounding try/catch blocks will never catch errors from these operations, and code after the forEach runs before the async operations complete.

The PR correctly fixes the async .forEach() pattern in one spot (handleCancelBooking.ts:474-480) by converting to a for...of loop, but introduces the exact same bug in the other four locations. The fix needs to be applied consistently.

The appStore object calls import() immediately for all 28 entries when the module is evaluated, firing them all in parallel eagerly rather than lazily. If the goal is deferred/lazy loading, the imports should be wrapped in thunks (arrow functions) instead of being invoked directly.

In CalendarManager.ts:28, getCalendarCredentials calls getCalendar(credential) synchronously, but getCalendar is now async, so the calendar field in the returned data structure is secretly a Promise rather than the resolved value. This is an implicit type change that acts as a landmine for future consumers of this data.
CalendarManager.ts:31 has a random blank line added that is pure noise in the diff.
